252.reason: [Unrecognized single-character symbol should trigger an error, but current code does nothing in 'default' case, leading to missing error reporting for invalid symbols]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the code correctly detects an error, but it still proceeds to output the erroneous token to lexer.txt via printToLexer at the end of getToken(), violating the requirement that erroneous programs should only produce error.txt and no lexer.txt]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This line outputs tokens even when a lexical error has been detected (e.g., single '&' or '|'), which violates the specification: if any lexical error exists, only error.txt should be produced and lexer.txt must be deleted and not written to. However, this print happens before the error flag is used to delete lexer.txt, so erroneous tokens are still written.]
